name: East Star Bus Co
districts_or_customers: Unknown - likely Oklahoma City area school/charter routes; no district contract publicly confirmed
recent_news: No public news or M&A activity found; very low online footprint
fit_notes: Sub-scale OKC single-yard operator (~19 employees, ~$818K revenue). Out of Summit's current footprint with weak public profile - likely too small and too opaque to prioritize as a tuck-in absent referral or relationship lead.

name: Lenz Bus Co Inc
principals: Trevor Lenz (Owner); historically associated with Johnson & Lenz Bus Company
districts_or_customers: Historically Sauk Prairie School District (19 years) - LOST contract to Lamers Bus Lines effective Aug 1, 2018 ($1.57M contract). Current customer base unclear post-loss.
recent_news: 2018: Lost long-running Sauk Prairie SD contract to Lamers Bus Lines; collaborated on transition. No indication of subsequent district wins.
fit_notes: Family-owned WI operator that lost anchor district contract to Lamers in 2018 - significantly diminished as a school-bus platform asset. May still operate charter/activity work but unlikely to be an attractive tuck-in unless rebuilt new contracts; succession + lost-contract context could make ownership receptive to exit at low multiple.

name: Defea School Bus Garage
principals: Pat DeFea (Owner); Steph DeFea (co-owner, wife); Mark DeFea (brother)
districts_or_customers: Milbank School District (primary, 489 sq mi, 10 routes, ~1,000 daily miles); also Grant-Deuel School District
recent_news: 2022 (Valley Express): Pat DeFea profiled - has run business since buying from Henze family in 1999 (Henzes founded 1964); signed 5-year district contract; explicitly stated preference for LOCAL ownership if he sells, noting larger companies acquiring similar operators in the region.
fit_notes: Clean family-owned tuck-in profile: 60-yr operating history, 15-bus fleet, two stable SD district contracts, owner near retirement age with a public five-year contract horizon. CAVEAT: owner publicly voiced preference for LOCAL successor over large consolidators - Summit would need careful relationship-led approach. Small scale (~$764K revenue) and remote SD geography limit strategic value vs. relationship/optionality play.

name: Real Bus Lines LLC
districts_or_customers: Unknown - Alpine School District serves Eagle Mountain area but no confirmed contract relationship found
recent_news: No public news found; no website, phone, or lead exec in source data; minimal online footprint
fit_notes: Very thin profile - residential address (3576 N Canary Way) suggests micro/home-based operator. Unable to verify school-bus operating status. Not actionable as a tuck-in target without significant additional diligence.

name: Dworak Motor Co Inc
principals: Jeff Kassner (President per source data); Jeff Dworak and Leonard Dworak referenced as Dworak family principals in business records
districts_or_customers: Historically Kewaunee School District since 1939 - did NOT bid on most recent contract; district awarded to Lamers Bus Lines. Likely no longer a school-district contractor.
recent_news: ~2024-2025: Dworak Bus Service opted not to submit a bid for Kewaunee SD transportation contract after 86 years (since 1939) of service; district awarded contract to Lamers Bus Lines and plans new district-owned bus facility. USDOT #366969 is now INACTIVE per SAFER database.
fit_notes: NOT A VIABLE TUCK-IN: Century-old family operator that effectively exited school-bus contracting - did not bid on its anchor Kewaunee SD contract and FMCSA USDOT registration is now INACTIVE. Business appears wound down or transitioning out of student transportation. De-prioritize.
